task_input: I have three orphan pages that all link to each other. Do I need to link to pages outside this group as well?
expected_behavior: Should explain that yes, they need to break out of the circular group. Each orphan must have outgoing links to OTHER pages, and those pages must link back. Circular-only linking between orphans won't resolve the orphan status.

task_input: I'm seeing multiple orphans in wiki_lint output. Should I fix them all at once or one at a time?
expected_behavior: Should recommend fixing them systematically, one at a time or in small batches, verifying with wiki_lint after each fix. Also should note that orphans may be related and fixing one might affect another's status.

task_input: My wiki_lint output shows 'README.md' as an orphan with 0 incoming and 0 outgoing links. I've added three different wiki links from other pages to README.md. Is it fixed?
expected_behavior: Should indicate this partial fix is not sufficient. While README.md now has incoming links, it still has 0 outgoing links. Must add a 'Related' or similar section with outbound wiki links FROM README.md.

task_input: I created a brand new wiki page and immediately wiki_lint shows it as an orphan before anyone links to it. Is this the expected behavior?
expected_behavior: Should confirm this is expected. A new page starts with 0 outgoing and 0 incoming links. The creator must: (1) Add outbound links from the new page, (2) Have other pages link to it, then verify with wiki_lint.

task_input: I added a link to 'api-reference.md' in my new page but wiki_lint still shows api-reference.md as an orphan. I thought adding any link to it would fix it.
expected_behavior: Should clarify that adding links TO a page doesn't create outgoing links FROM that page. api-reference.md itself must contain outbound links. The new page adding a link only helps with the incoming count.
